author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #587. That issue added \`load_safetensors_mmap\` and \`load_safetensors_sharded_mmap\` (memmap2-backed) to halve peak RSS at LLM scale. The other two formats listed in the original issue title still need the same treatment:

## GGUF (\`ferrotorch-serialize/src/gguf.rs\`)
GGUF is similar to safetensors — header + raw tensor blocks — so the mmap path is straightforward:
1. \`File::open(...)\` then \`unsafe { Mmap::map(&file) }\`.
2. Parse the GGUF header from \`&mmap[..]\`.
3. For each tensor: slice into the mmap region for that tensor's offset+size and run the existing dequant / cast paths into owned \`Vec<T>\`.

The savings are bigger on GGUF because GGUF often holds quantized blocks that need to be expanded — the input bytes are 1/2 to 1/8 of the output tensor size, so doubling them in heap is comparatively expensive.

## pytorch-pickle (\`pytorch_import.rs\`)
Pickle archives are zip files with embedded pickle metadata + raw tensor blobs. Mmap'ing the zip is possible (\`zip::ZipArchive::new(Cursor::new(&mmap[..]))\`) but more involved because:
- pickle parsing wants \`Read\` on a sub-file, not a borrowed slice
- the existing \`load_pytorch_state_dict\` allocates aggressively during pickle interpretation

A shallower win is just to avoid \`std::fs::read\` on the outer zip — even if the pickle parser still allocates internally.

## Notes
- Same safety contract as #587: mmap dropped before the loader returns, no borrows escape, file must not be mutated while live.
- For huge models, the mmap variants should be the default in \`load_<format>_auto\`, with a feature flag or env var fallback to the read-into-Vec path on platforms with broken mmap (rare on modern Linux/macOS/Windows).
